G5125.derivation: dative case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5125.strongs_def: to (for, in, with or by) these (persons or things)
G5125.kjv_def: such, them, there(-in, -with), these, this, those
G5125.usage: these(7x), this(2x), such(1x), them(1x), therein(1x), therewith(1x), those(1x)

G5134.strongs_def: akin to the base of G2352 (θραύω), G5147 (τρίβος), G5149 (τρίζω), etc.); a wound

G5190.strongs_def: fortuitous, i.e. fortunate; Tychicus, a Christian

G5115.strongs_def: a bow (apparently as the simplest fabric)

G5184.derivation: of Hebrew origin (H06865): Tyrus (i.e. Tsor), a place in Palestine

G5100.strongs_def: some or any person or object
G5100.kjv_def: a (kind of), any (man, thing, thing at all), certain (thing), divers, he (every) man, one (X thing), ought, + partly, some (man, -body, - thing, -what), (+ that no-)thing, what(-soever), X wherewith, whom(-soever), whose(-soever)
G5100.usage: certain(104x), some(73x), any(37x), one(34x), man(34x), anything(24x), a(9x), something(6x), somewhat(6x), ought(5x), divers(2x), he(2x), thing(1x), another(2x)

G5150.derivation: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G3376 (μήν) as noun;

G5129.derivation: dative case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5129.strongs_def: to (in, with or by) this (person or thing)
G5129.kjv_def: here(-by, -in), him, one, the same, there(-in), this

G5104.derivation: probably for the dative case of G3588 (ὁ);
G5104.strongs_def: an enclitic particle of asseveration by way of contrast; in sooth
G5104.kjv_def: (used only with other particles in the comparative, as G2544 (καίτοιγε), G3305 (μέντοι), G5105 (τοιγαροῦν), G5106 (τοίνυν), etc

G5172.derivation: from (to break up or (figuratively) enfeeble, especially the mind and body by indulgence);

G5168.derivation: from a derivative of (to wear away;
G5168.strongs_def: akin to the base of G5134 (τραῦμα), G5147 (τρίβος) and G5176 (τρώγω)); an orifice, i.e. needle's eye

G5130.strongs_def: of (from or concerning) these (persons or things)
G5130.kjv_def: such, their, these (things), they, this sort, those

G5167.strongs_def: akin to G5149 (τρίζω), but denoting a duller sound); a turtle-dove (as cooing)

G5110.strongs_def: interest on money loaned (as a produce)

G5138.derivation: perhaps strengthened from the base of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι) (as if jagged by rents);

G5149.strongs_def: to creak (squeak), i.e. (by analogy) to grate the teeth (in frenzy)

G5124.derivation: neuter singular nominative or accusative case of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5124.kjv_def: here (-unto), it, partly, self(-same), so, that (intent), the same, there(-fore, -unto), this, thus, where(-fore)

G5199.strongs_def: healthy, i.e. well (in body); figuratively, true (in doctrine)

G5182.strongs_def: akin to G2351 (θόρυβος)); to make "turbid", i.e. disturb

G5176.derivation: probably strengthened from a collateral form of the base of G5134 (τραῦμα) and G5147 (τρίβος) through the idea of corrosion or wear;
G5176.strongs_def: or perhaps rather of a base of G5167 (τρυγών) and G5149 (τρίζω) through the idea of a craunching sound; to gnaw or chew, i.e. (generally) to eat

G5185.strongs_def: opaque (as if smoky), i.e. (by analogy) blind (physically or mentally)

G5143.strongs_def: compare G2359 (θρίξ)); which uses (the base of G1408 (δρόμος)) as alternate in certain tenses; to run or walk hastily (literally or figuratively)

G5119.derivation: from (the neuter of) G3588 (ὁ) and G3753 (ὅτε);
G5119.strongs_def: the when, i.e. at the time that (of the past or future, also in consecution)

G5178.derivation: from a derivative of G5180 (τύπτω) (meaning a drum, "tympanum");
G5178.strongs_def: to stretch on an instrument of torture resembling a drum, and thus beat to death

G5177.derivation: probably for an obsolete (for which the middle voice of another alternate (to make ready or bring to pass) is used in certain tenses;
G5177.strongs_def: akin to the base of G5088 (τίκτω) through the idea of effecting; properly, to affect; or (specially), to hit or light upon (as a mark to be reached), i.e. (transitively) to attain or secure an object or end, or (intransitively) to happen (as if meeting with); but in the latter application only impersonal (with G1487 (εἰ)), i.e. perchance; or (present participle) as adjective, usual (as if commonly met with, with G3756 (οὐ), extraordinary), neuter (as adverb) perhaps; or (with another verb) as adverb, by accident (as it were)
G5177.kjv_def: be, chance, enjoy, little, obtain, X refresh…self, + special

G5194.derivation: perhaps from the same as G5205 (ὑετός) (as being transparent like rain);

G5107.derivation: (including the other inflections); from a derivative of 5104 and G1161 (δέ);

G5174.strongs_def: the Troad (or plain of Troy), i.e. Troas, a place in Asia Minor

G5146.strongs_def: properly, a crow-foot (three-pronged obstruction in war), i.e. (by analogy) a thorny plant (caltrop)

G5117.strongs_def: a spot (general in space, but limited by occupancy; whereas G5561 (χώρα) is a large but participle locality), i.e. location (as a position, home, tract, etc.); figuratively, condition, opportunity; specially, a scabbard
G5117.kjv_def: coast, licence, place, X plain, quarter, + rock, room, where
G5117.usage: place(80x), room(5x), quarter(2x), licence(1x), coast(1x), where(1x)

G5164.strongs_def: a wheel (as a runner), i.e. (figuratively) a circuit of physical effects

G5157.derivation: from an apparently primary to turn;

G5121.derivation: contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G1726 (ἐναντίον);

G5152.derivation: neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G4721 (στέγη) as noun;

G5148.derivation: from a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G2094 (ἔτος);

G5142.strongs_def: but perhaps strengthened from the base of G5157 (τροπή) through the idea of convolution); properly, to stiffen, i.e. fatten (by implication, to cherish (with food, etc.), pamper, rear)

G5187.strongs_def: to envelop with smoke, i.e. (figuratively) to inflate with self-conceit
G5187.kjv_def: high-minded, be lifted up with pride, be proud

G5186.strongs_def: to make blind, i.e. (figuratively) to obscure

G5105.derivation: from G5104 (τοί) and G1063 (γάρ) and G3767 (οὖν);

G5169.derivation: from a derivative of the base of G5168 (τρυμαλιά);
G5169.strongs_def: an aperture, i.e. a needle's eye

G5139.strongs_def: rough district; Trachonitis, a region of Syria

G5122.derivation: contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G3686 (ὄνομα);

G5181.derivation: a provincial form of the derivative of the base of G2962 (κύριος);

G5179.strongs_def: a die (as struck), i.e. (by implication) a stamp or scar; by analogy, a shape, i.e. a statue, (figuratively) style or resemblance; specially, a sampler ("type"), i.e. a model (for imitation) or instance (for warning)
G5179.kjv_def: en-(ex-)ample, fashion, figure, form, manner, pattern, print
G5179.usage: ensample(5x), print(2x), figure(2x), example(2x), pattern(2x), fashion(1x), manner(1x), form(1x)

G5198.strongs_def: to have sound health, i.e. be well (in body); figuratively, to be uncorrupt (true in doctrine)
G5198.kjv_def: be in health, (be safe and) sound, (be) whole(-some)

G5114.derivation: comparative of a derivative of the primary (to cut;
G5114.strongs_def: more comprehensive or decisive than G2875 (κόπτω), as if by a single stroke; whereas that implies repeated blows, like hacking); more keen

G5120.derivation: properly, the genitive case of G3588 (ὁ);
G5120.strongs_def: sometimes used for G5127 (τούτου); of this person

G5126.derivation: accusative case singular masculine of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5126.strongs_def: this (person, as objective of verb or preposition)

G5154.strongs_def: third; neuter (as noun) a third part, or (as adverb) a (or the) third time, thirdly

G5183.strongs_def: a Tyrian, i.e. inhabitant of Tyrus

G5136.strongs_def: to seize by the throat or neck, i.e. to expose the gullet of a victim for killing (generally, to lay bare)

G5188.strongs_def: to make a smoke, i.e. slowly consume without flame

G5132.derivation: probably contracted from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3979 (πεζῇ);
G5132.strongs_def: a table or stool (as being four-legged), usually for food (figuratively, a meal); also a counter for money (figuratively, a broker's office for loans at interest)

G5111.strongs_def: probably itself from the base of G5056 (τέλος) through the idea of extreme conduct); to venture (objectively or in act; while G2292 (θαῤῥέω) is rather subjective or in feeling); by implication, to be courageous

G5127.derivation: genitive case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5127.strongs_def: of (from or concerning) this (person or thing)
G5127.kjv_def: here(-by), him, it, + such manner of, that, thence(-forth), thereabout, this, thus
G5127.usage: this(64x), that(4x), him(2x), thus(1x), it(1x), thenceforth(1x)

G5116.derivation: neuter of a presumed derivative (alternately) of (a "topaz";
G5116.strongs_def: of uncertain origin); a gem, probably the chrysolite

G5192.strongs_def: the "hyacinth" or "jacinth", i.e. some gem of a deep blue color, probably the zirkon

G5101.strongs_def: an interrogative pronoun, who, which or what (in direct or indirect questions)
G5101.kjv_def: every man, how (much), + no(-ne, thing), what (manner, thing), where (-by, -fore, -of, -unto, - with, -withal), whether, which, who(-m, -se), why

G5108.derivation: (including the other inflections); from G5104 (τοί) and G3778 (οὗτος);
G5108.strongs_def: truly this, i.e. of this sort (to denote character or individuality)

G5163.strongs_def: a track (as a wheel-rut), i.e. (figuratively) a course of conduct

G5180.derivation: a primary verb (in a strengthened form);
G5180.strongs_def: to "thump", i.e. cudgel or pummel (properly, with a stick or bastinado), but in any case by repeated blows; thus differing from G3817 (παίω) and G3960 (πατάσσω), which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 (πλήσσω) with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 (ῥαπίζω) with the palm; as well as from G5177 (τυγχάνω), an accidental collision); by implication, to punish; figuratively, to offend (the conscience)

G5166.derivation: from a derivative of (to dry) meaning ripe fruit (as if dry);

G5103.derivation: of Latin origin but uncertain significance;

G5147.strongs_def: akin to , , and the base of G5131 (τράγος), G5134 (τραῦμα)); a rut or worn track

G5118.strongs_def: apparently from G3588 (ὁ) and G3739 (ὅς)) and G3778 (οὗτος) (including its variations); so vast as this, i.e. such (in quantity, amount, number of space)
G5118.kjv_def: as large, so great (long, many, much), these many

G5175.strongs_def: Trogyllium, a place in Asia Minor

G5128.derivation: accusative case plural masculine of G3778 (οὗτος);
G5128.strongs_def: these (persons, as objective of verb or preposition)

G5165.derivation: neuter of a presumed derivative of uncertain affinity;

G5160.strongs_def: nourishment (literally or figuratively); by implication, rations (wages)

G5158.strongs_def: a turn, i.e. (by implication) mode or style (especially with preposition or relative prefix as adverb, like); figuratively, deportment or character
G5158.kjv_def: (even) as, conversation, (+ like) manner, (+ by any) means, way

G5112.derivation: neuter of the compound of a derivative of the base of 5111 (as adverb);
G5112.strongs_def: more daringly, i.e. with greater confidence than otherwise

G5137.derivation: probably from G5143 (τρέχω) (through the idea of mobility);
